stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
The illustration depicts two aircraft, namely aeroplane 1 and aeroplane 2, both equipped with an identical fly-by-wire system designed to consistently uphold the aircraft's trajectory unless manually altered by the pilot or autopilot. However, following sensor failures in each aircraft, the fly-by-wire systems undergo a downgrade, leading the aeroplanes to switch to a conventional-style backup control. In this mode, there is a direct correlation between the control column position and the corresponding control surface position. What implications does this have for the pilots?

options.a: Both aeroplanes 1 and 2 will have similar behaviour since they are statically stable and in both cases the aeroplane will return to its trimmed position after configuration or thrust changes.
options.b: Aeroplane 1 will feel significantly different in pitch with large trim changes as a result of thrust changes, while aeroplane 2 may suffer control reversal and loss of control feel.
options.c: Both aeroplanes 1 and 2 will have significantly different behaviour and control feel due to the absence of a computer compensating for the effects of changing configuration or thrust.
options.d: Aeroplane 1 will behave and feel very similar to normal, while aeroplane 2 will have significant changes in pitch control and feel, with a risk of entering pilot-induced oscillations.
